edges.description: Reduced GAMT activity blocks conversion of guanidinoacetate to creatine.
Blocked methylation leaves guanidinoacetate upstream of GAMT to accumulate to neurotoxic levels.
Cerebral creatine depletion compromises the creatine/phosphocreatine ATP-buffering system in brain.
Failure to convert guanidinoacetate to creatine causes elevated guanidinoacetate in biofluids and brain.
Failure of the final creatine biosynthesis step lowers systemic and cerebral creatine.
Cerebral creatine depletion is detected as reduced creatine on proton brain MRS.
Low creatinine reflects reduced creatine available for spontaneous conversion to creatinine.
intermediate_mechanisms: Reduced creatine availability lowers downstream creatinine generation.

edges.description: Guanidinoacetate toxicity compounds the neuronal energy deficit from cerebral creatine depletion.
intermediate_mechanisms: Elevated guanidinoacetate perturbs neuronal metabolism and inhibitory neurotransmission.

edges.description: Guanidinoacetate toxicity contributes to the clinical neurologic phenotype.
intermediate_mechanisms: Elevated guanidinoacetate contributes to neuronal dysfunction alongside cerebral creatine depletion.

edges.description: Neuronal energy disruption contributes to developmental, seizure, speech, behavioral, and motor manifestations.
intermediate_mechanisms: Loss of the creatine/phosphocreatine ATP buffer affects high-energy neural systems.

edges.description: Developmental delay is part of the core neurodevelopmental expression of GAMT deficiency.
Intellectual disability is a core clinical result of cerebral creatine depletion and GAA accumulation.
Speech and language delay are prominent neurodevelopmental manifestations.
Absent speech represents the severe end of GAMT-associated speech and language impairment.
intermediate_mechanisms: Severe limited speech development can progress to absent or extremely limited speech.

edges.description: Seizures are a core manifestation of the combined creatine-depletion and GAA-toxicity neurologic phenotype.
Behavioral problems are among the dominant clinical domains in GAMT deficiency.
Autistic behavior is a reported subset of GAMT-associated behavioral abnormalities.
intermediate_mechanisms: Autistic features are part of the broader behavioral phenotype.

edges.description: Movement disorder is part of the reported neurologic presentation.
Ataxia is one of the reported GAMT-associated movement abnormalities.
intermediate_mechanisms: basal ganglia and broader motor-system dysfunction

edges.description: Dystonia is one of the reported GAMT-associated movement abnormalities.
intermediate_mechanisms: basal ganglia and broader motor-system dysfunction

edges.description: Hypotonia is part of the untreated neurologic presentation.
Involuntary movements are reported among untreated neurologic features.
Globus pallidus signal abnormality reflects basal ganglia involvement in GAMT deficiency.
Hyperactivity is part of the GAMT behavioral phenotype spectrum.
intermediate_mechanisms: broader behavioral dysregulation in GAMT deficiency

edges.description: Aggressive behavior is part of the GAMT behavioral phenotype spectrum.
intermediate_mechanisms: broader behavioral dysregulation in GAMT deficiency

edges.description: Self-injurious behavior is part of the GAMT behavioral phenotype spectrum.
intermediate_mechanisms: broader behavioral dysregulation in GAMT deficiency

edges.description: Elevated guanidinoacetate reports the blocked GAMT-dependent conversion of guanidinoacetate to creatine.
Plasma, CSF, urine, or brain guanidinoacetate tracks the toxic upstream metabolite burden targeted by substrate-reduction therapy.
Low creatine reports the missing product of the blocked GAMT-dependent creatine biosynthetic step.
Persistently low brain creatine on spectroscopy tracks the residual creatine/phosphocreatine energy-buffer deficit during treatment.
Brain MRS creatine is a tissue-level readout of cerebral creatine repletion and remaining neuronal energy-buffer deficiency.

nodes.description: MRI shows increased signal in the globus pallidus, and T2 hyperintensities in the basal ganglia have been reported.

evidence.reference_title: Guanidinoacetate methyltransferase deficiency (GAMT).
evidence.snippet: The MRI scan shows an increased signal in the globus pallidus, and the diagnosis is confirmed by finding increased guanidinoacetate in the urine and a low plasma creatine.

nodes.description: In severe cases, patients may have absent or extremely limited speech production, representing the severe end of the speech/language impairment spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Evidence and Recommendation for Guanidinoacetate Methyltransferase Deficiency Newborn Screening.
evidence.snippet: Untreated, GAMT deficiency is associated with hypotonia, significant intellectual disability, limited speech development, recurrent seizures, behavior problems, and involuntary movements.

nodes.description: Aggressive behavior is a specific behavioral phenotype within the GAMT behavioral spectrum.

evidence.reference_title: Guanidinoacetate methyltransferase deficiency (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0000718 | Aggressive behavior | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Antiepileptic medications are used to manage seizures, which affect approximately 70% of patients. Seizure improvement is often observed with metabolic treatment (creatine supplementation), sometimes allowing reduction of anticonvulsant medications.

evidence.reference_title: Guanidinoacetate methyltransferase deficiency (GAMT).
evidence.snippet: This results in improvement of many of the symptoms, especially of the epileptic seizures and the abnormal movements.

nodes.description: Ataxia is a more specific movement phenotype reported in GAMT deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Guanidinoacetate methyltransferase deficiency (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0001251 | Ataxia | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Autistic features and autistic-like behavior are reported in a subset of patients with GAMT deficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Guanidinoacetate methyltransferase deficiency (GAMT).
evidence.snippet: Other symptoms include learning disorders, autistic behaviour, epileptic seizures, and movement disorders.

nodes.description: Behavioral disturbances are common and include autistic features, self-mutilation, hyperactivity, and other severe behavioral disorders.

evidence.reference_title: Guanidinoacetate methyltransferase deficiency (GAMT).
evidence.snippet: Other symptoms include learning disorders, autistic behaviour, epileptic seizures, and movement disorders.

evidence.snippet: Patients with these disorders present with a wide range of symptoms, including developmental delay, seizures, movement disorder, behavioral problems, and hypotonia.

evidence.snippet: the creatine concentrations, which were regularly monitored over a long follow-up period, increased significantly over time, but did not reach age matched control ranges.

evidence.reference_title: Creatine Deficiency Disorders: Phenotypes, Genotypes, Diagnosis, and Treatment Outcomes.
evidence.snippet: The diagnosis can be suspected by elevated guanidinoacetate and low creatine levels in body fluids in guanidinoacetate methyltransferase deficiency

Persistently low brain creatine on spectroscopy tracks the residual creatine/phosphocreatine energy-buffer deficit during treatment.

evidence.reference_title: Long term follow-up in GAMT deficiency - Correlation of therapy regimen, biochemical and in vivo brain proton MR spectroscopy data.
evidence.snippet: the creatine concentrations, which were regularly monitored over a long follow-up period, increased significantly over time, but did not reach age matched control ranges.

readouts.interpretation: Brain MRS creatine is a tissue-level readout of cerebral creatine repletion and remaining neuronal energy-buffer deficiency.

nodes.description: Oral creatine monohydrate is the cornerstone of therapy, aimed at repleting systemic and cerebral creatine stores. Typical dosing is approximately 400-800 mg/kg/day. Treatment is associated with improvement of cerebral creatine on MRS and clinical symptom stabilization or improvement, though brain creatine may not fully normalize.

evidence.reference_title: Guanidinoacetate methyltransferase (GAMT) deficiency: outcomes in 48 individuals and recommendations for diagnosis, treatment and monitoring.
evidence.snippet: Increase of cerebral creatine and decrease of plasma/CSF guanidinoacetate levels were achieved by supplementation with creatine-monohydrate combined with high dosages of l-ornithine and/or an arginine-restricted diet

evidence.reference_title: Guanidinoacetate methyltransferase (GAMT) deficiency: outcomes in 48 individuals and recommendations for diagnosis, treatment and monitoring.
evidence.snippet: Therapy was associated with improvement or stabilization of symptoms in all of the symptomatic cases. The 4 patients treated younger than 9 months had normal or almost normal developmental outcomes.

evidence.reference_title: Long term follow-up in GAMT deficiency - Correlation of therapy regimen, biochemical and in vivo brain proton MR spectroscopy data.
evidence.snippet: Treatment consists of daily creatine supplementation to increase cerebral creatine, reduction of arginine intake and supplementation of ornithine for reduction of toxic GAA levels.

nodes.description: Blocked conversion of guanidinoacetate to creatine causes systemic and cerebral creatine depletion, impairing the creatine/phosphocreatine energy shuttle needed for rapid ATP regeneration in high-energy-demand tissues.

evidence.reference_title: Long term follow-up in GAMT deficiency - Correlation of therapy regimen, biochemical and in vivo brain proton MR spectroscopy data.
evidence.snippet: Cerebral creatine depletion and accumulation of guanidinoacetate (GAA) lead to clinical presentation with intellectual disability, seizures, speech disturbances and movement disorders.

nodes.description: Speech and language delay is particularly prominent and is often the most affected developmental domain. Many patients have severely limited or absent speech.

evidence.reference_title: Guanidinoacetate methyltransferase deficiency (GAMT).
evidence.snippet: The symptoms and signs are reviewed, emphasising that delayed language development is a particular feature.

evidence.reference_title: Guanidinoacetate methyltransferase (GAMT) deficiency: outcomes in 48 individuals and recommendations for diagnosis, treatment and monitoring.
evidence.snippet: Global developmental delay/intellectual disability (DD/ID) with speech/language delay and behavioral problems as the most affected domains was present in 44 participants

nodes.description: Dystonia is a more specific extrapyramidal movement phenotype reported in GAMT deficiency.

evidence.snippet: HP:0001332 | Dystonia | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Biallelic pathogenic variants in GAMT reduce guanidinoacetate methyltransferase catalytic activity.

evidence.snippet: Guanidinoacetate methyltransferase (GAMT) deficiency is an autosomal recessive disorder of creatine biosynthesis due to pathogenic variants in the GAMT gene that lead to cerebral creatine deficiency and neurotoxic levels of guanidinoacetate.

evidence.snippet: Guanidinoacetate methyltransferase deficiency (GAMT) is an autosomal recessive inborn error of metabolism. A condition that results from a pathogenic variant in the GAMT gene that maps to 19p13.3.

evidence.reference_title: GAMT / guanidinoacetate methyltransferase deficiency (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: GAMT | HGNC:4136 | guanidinoacetate methyltransferase deficiency | MONDO:0012999 | AR | Definitive

nodes.description: AAV-based gene therapy expressing human GAMT in hepatocytes is under preclinical development. In a murine model, liver-directed gene therapy achieved pan-hepatic GAMT expression, marked and sustained reduction of GAA, normalization of plasma creatine, increased cerebral and myocardial creatine, and resolution of behavioral abnormalities. CNS-directed AAV9 approaches are also being investigated.

evidence.reference_title: Gene therapy for guanidinoacetate methyltransferase deficiency restores cerebral and myocardial creatine while resolving behavioral abnormalities.
evidence.snippet: Serial collection of blood demonstrated a marked early and sustained reduction of GAA with normalization of plasma creatine; urinary GAA levels also markedly declined.

evidence.reference_title: Gene therapy for guanidinoacetate methyltransferase deficiency restores cerebral and myocardial creatine while resolving behavioral abnormalities.
evidence.snippet: The terminal time point demonstrated marked improvement in cerebral and myocardial creatine levels. In conjunction with the biochemical findings, treated mice gained weight to nearly match their wild-type littermates, while behavioral studies demonstrated resolution of abnormalities

nodes.description: Developmental delay/intellectual disability is the most consistent feature, present in 44 of 48 patients (92%) in the landmark cohort, 100% in a 2023 review, and 84% in a 53-case scoping review.

nodes.description: Guanidinoacetate (GAA) accumulates in brain, CSF, blood, and urine due to blocked conversion to creatine. GAA is neurotoxic and may alter inhibitory neurotransmission, compounding the effects of cerebral energy deficiency.

nodes.description: The creatine/phosphocreatine system serves as a critical ATP buffer and energy shuttle in neurons. Creatine deficiency impairs rapid ATP regeneration during fluctuating energy demands, contributing to neurodevelopmental dysfunction, seizure susceptibility, and disrupted neurite outgrowth and neurotransmission.

evidence.reference_title: Evidence and Recommendation for Guanidinoacetate Methyltransferase Deficiency Newborn Screening.

evidence.snippet: Creatine deficiency disorders are inborn errors of creatine metabolism, an energy homeostasis molecule.

nodes.description: Intellectual disability ranges from mild to severe and is a hallmark feature. Severity correlates with age at treatment initiation, with patients treated before 9 months achieving normal or near-normal cognitive outcomes.

evidence.reference_title: Guanidinoacetate methyltransferase (GAMT) deficiency: outcomes in 48 individuals and recommendations for diagnosis, treatment and monitoring.
evidence.snippet: The median age at treatment initiation was 25.5 and 39 months in patients with mild and moderate DD/ID, respectively, and 11 years in patients with severe DD/ID.

evidence.snippet: 43% of the cases had intellectual disabilities and mental retardation that affected their learning process; most cases required special care.

nodes.description: GAMT deficiency screening can be incorporated into existing tandem mass spectrometry newborn screening platforms, detecting elevated guanidinoacetate. The Advisory Committee on Heritable Disorders in Newborns and Children recommended addition to the RUSP, accepted in January 2023. Presymptomatic detection enables early treatment initiation before irreversible neurologic damage.

evidence.reference_title: Evidence and Recommendation for Guanidinoacetate Methyltransferase Deficiency Newborn Screening.
evidence.snippet: the feasibility of screening, the low number of positive results, the relative ease of diagnosis, and the expected benefit of presymptomatic dietary therapy led to a recommendation from the Advisory Committee on Heritable Disorders in Newborns and Children to the Secretary of Health and Human Services that GAMT deficiency be added to the Recommended Uniform Screening Panel. This recommendation was accepted in January 2023.

nodes.description: Oral L-ornithine is used as a substrate-reduction strategy to inhibit AGAT activity, thereby reducing guanidinoacetate synthesis. Dosing ranges from 100 to 800 mg/kg/day. Ornithine combined with creatine is more effective than creatine alone at reducing GAA levels.

evidence.reference_title: Guanidinoacetate methyltransferase (GAMT) deficiency: outcomes in 48 individuals and recommendations for diagnosis, treatment and monitoring.
evidence.snippet: Treatment regimens included various combinations/dosages of creatine-monohydrate, l-ornithine, sodium benzoate and protein/arginine restricted diets.

evidence.reference_title: Guanidinoacetate methyltransferase (GAMT) deficiency: outcomes in 48 individuals and recommendations for diagnosis, treatment and monitoring.
evidence.snippet: Increase of cerebral creatine and decrease of plasma/CSF guanidinoacetate levels were achieved by supplementation with creatine-monohydrate combined with high dosages of l-ornithine and/or an arginine-restricted diet

evidence.reference_title: Long term follow-up in GAMT deficiency - Correlation of therapy regimen, biochemical and in vivo brain proton MR spectroscopy data.
evidence.snippet: Treatment consists of daily creatine supplementation to increase cerebral creatine, reduction of arginine intake and supplementation of ornithine for reduction of toxic GAA levels.

nodes.description: Dietary restriction of protein and/or arginine intake reduces the precursor supply for guanidinoacetate synthesis via AGAT, serving as a substrate-reduction approach. This is typically combined with creatine and ornithine supplementation.

evidence.reference_title: Guanidinoacetate methyltransferase (GAMT) deficiency: outcomes in 48 individuals and recommendations for diagnosis, treatment and monitoring.
evidence.snippet: Increase of cerebral creatine and decrease of plasma/CSF guanidinoacetate levels were achieved by supplementation with creatine-monohydrate combined with high dosages of l-ornithine and/or an arginine-restricted diet (250 mg/kg/d l-arginine).

evidence.reference_title: Guanidinoacetate methyltransferase deficiency (GAMT).
evidence.snippet: Treatment is based on giving creatine supplementation orally and a low-protein diet with restricted arginine and increased ornithine.

nodes.description: Epilepsy occurs in approximately 70-73% of patients. Seizures may reflect both cerebral energy instability and direct GAA effects on inhibitory neurotransmission. Seizure control often improves with creatine supplementation.

evidence.snippet: Global developmental delay/intellectual disability (DD/ID) with speech/language delay and behavioral problems as the most affected domains was present in 44 participants, with additional epilepsy present in 35 and movement disorder in 13.

evidence.snippet: 68% of the cases developed generalized seizures throughout their life.

nodes.description: Sodium benzoate is used in some treatment protocols to reduce glycine availability, a precursor for guanidinoacetate synthesis. Dosing is approximately 100 mg/kg/day. This reduces the substrate pool available for AGAT, contributing to GAA lowering.

evidence.snippet: Treatment regimens included various combinations/dosages of creatine-monohydrate, l-ornithine, sodium benzoate and protein/arginine restricted diets.
